concerns.text: T014.6 implements 'hold-out set logic' but fails to explicitly mandate the generation of the 'standard linear trajectories' dataset (T019.5) as a disjoint training set for the Baseline agent, violating FR-001's requirement that 'The Baseline agent must be trained on standard linear trajectories ONLY' and that the benchmark uses a 'hold-out set of error patterns disjoint from any used in the Hybrid agent's training data'.

T030 implements 'sensitivity analysis logic' but does not explicitly mandate the execution of the specific 'perturbation runs' (varying error-injection thresholds by ±10%) required by SC-005 to verify the <5% stability criterion. The task describes the logic but omits the mandatory execution step to produce the verification data.

FR-008 requires validating synthetic error patterns against a 'documented real-world error taxonomy' to ensure external validity. T014.5 loads the taxonomy and T013 verifies citations, but no task explicitly implements the 'validation logic' that maps generated tasks to taxonomy rules to prove this external validity, leaving the FR-008 acceptance criterion unverified.

FR-006 mandates a 'maximum step limit of 50 actions per task' to prevent infinite loops. T024 mentions 'infinite loop detection' but does not explicitly mandate the enforcement of the 50-step hard limit as a specific code path in the runner, relying instead on configuration which may not be actively enforced, risking a violation of the hard constraint.

The Edge Cases section of spec.md mandates that if the CPU-only model exceeds the 6-hour time limit, the 'System must abort and report timeout, not hang'. While T025 implements a pilot run, there is no explicit task to implement the 'automatic abort and report timeout' mechanism for the main evaluation run, leaving SC-004 (≤6 hours) unenforceable in the implementation plan.
